decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing a combination of creatures with evasion like Squadron Hawk and Guardian of the Guildpact, along with powerful removal spells like Journey to Nowhere and Thraben Charm. The deck also includes card draw and counter spells to maintain card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The Gate lands allow for fixing mana and provide additional utility with cards like The Modern Age and Lórien Revealed. Overall, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition and value.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a quick and aggressive strategy using a variety of Elf creatures. By utilizing cards like Avenging Hunter, Priest of Titania, and Timberwatch Elf, the deck aims to generate large amounts of mana and pump up its creatures to deal massive damage quickly. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help to refill the hand with more creatures, ensuring a steady stream of threats. With a focus on Elves and mana production, this deck aims to outpace and outpower the opponent in a Pauper 1 vs 1 format.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly deploy aggressive creatures like Festival Crasher, Kiln Fiend, and Crackling Cyclops, and enhance them with spells like Apostle's Blessing and Mutagenic Growth to deal massive damage to the opponent. Manamorphose and Rite of Flame help to ramp up mana for explosive turns, while Ancestral Anger and Assault Strobe provide additional ways to pump up creatures for lethal attacks. The inclusion of cards like Ghitu Amplifier and Temur Battle Rage further amplify this aggro strategy, aiming to overwhelm and defeat the opponent before they can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to effectively control the game through card draw, counterspells, and removal spells while utilizing powerful threats like Cryptic Serpent and Tolarian Terror. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Counterspell and Force Spike, while also generating card advantage through spells like Brainstorm and Frantic Inventory. Additionally, the inclusion of Lórien Revealed assists in maintaining hand advantage, further solidifying the control aspect of the deck. The deck also includes some disruptive elements like Deem Inferior and Sleep of the Dead to hinder the opponent's board development.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the Balustrade Spy combo to mill your entire library and win the game. The deck includes cards that can help accelerate mana production, find key combo pieces, and protect your combo pieces from removal. The goal is to consistently execute the combo as early as possible to overwhelm your opponent and secure a victory. Additionally, the deck includes some flexibility and resilience with additional win conditions and ways to disrupt your opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of creature removal, counterspells, and card draw. It utilizes efficient removal spells like Snuff Out and Cast Down, alongside powerful counterspells like Counterspell and Force Spike to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes cards like Thought Scour and Lórien Revealed to help dig through the deck and find key pieces. Overall, the goal is to outvalue the opponent by efficiently controlling the board and generating card advantage.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent through aggressive creatures and burn spells. By playing efficient creatures like Kessig Flamebreather and Voldaren Epicure, as well as utilizing burn spells like Fiery Temper and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to reduce the opponent's life total as fast as possible. Additionally, cards like Guttersnipe and Fireblast provide additional reach and damage potential. The inclusion of cards like Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize help to ensure a steady stream of threats and resources to maintain pressure on the opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to apply constant pressure on the opponent by playing low-cost, evasive creatures like Brinebarrow Intruder, Faerie Miscreant, and Ninja of the Deep Hours. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Counterspell and Spellstutter Sprite to control the opponent's game plan. Additionally, cards like Shore Up and Bind the Monster help to further control the board while maintaining the aggressive strategy. The overall goal is to maintain a fast pace, out-tempo the opponent, and secure victory through early damage and disruption.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts to the battlefield and utilizing them to generate advantage and overwhelm the opponent. By combining efficient creatures like Myr Enforcer and Moon-Circuit Hacker with card draw and mana acceleration from cards like Springleaf Drum and Thoughtcast, the deck aims to apply continuous pressure and outpace the opponent. Additionally, the inclusion of Metallic Rebuke and Relic of Progenitus provides disruption and graveyard hate to control the pace of the game. Overall, the key concept is to swarm the board with artifacts and exploit their synergies to win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on the combo strategy of using Balustrade Spy to mill the entire library, followed by using Dread Return to reanimate a powerful creature like Lotleth Giant. The deck includes cards that can ramp up mana quickly and find the combo pieces, as well as a few disruption cards like Mesmeric Fiend. The goal is to execute the combo as quickly and efficiently as possible to overwhelm the opponent and secure the win.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to out-value the opponent and ultimately win the game. Key cards like Brainstorm, Counterspell, and Snuff Out help to maintain control over the game, while creatures like Gurmag Angler and Murmuring Mystic provide a win condition. The deck also includes efficient card draw and mana fixing to ensure consistency in its control strategy.

The main focus of this Pauper Dimir Control deck is to use a combination of counterspells, removal, and card draw to control the opponent's board and hand while establishing a win condition with creatures like Sneaky Snacker and Gurmag Angler. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while efficiently deploying threats of its own, ultimately outvaluing and outmaneuvering the opponent to secure a victory. It also utilizes graveyard synergies with cards like Thought Scour and Tolarian Terror to further bolster its game plan.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly deal damage to the opponent through aggressive creatures and burn spells. The strategy involves playing creatures that can attack early and consistently, while also using burn spells to target the opponent directly. Cards like Kessig Flamebreather, Sneaky Snacker, and Voldaren Epicure provide a strong aggressive presence, while Fiery Temper, Lava Dart, and Lightning Bolt allow for efficient damage output. Additionally, cards like Fireblast, Chain Lightning, and Highway Robbery offer additional reach to close out the game.

The main idea behind this Rakdos Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle. By playing efficient and cheap creatures like Kitchen Imp, Sneaky Snacker, and Voldaren Epicure, along with burn spells like Fiery Temper, Lightning Bolt, and Alms of the Vein, the deck aims to chip away at the opponent's life total quickly. The deck also includes cards like Faithless Looting and Grab the Prize to enable card draw and maintain pressure on the opponent. Overall, the key strategy is to be fast, aggressive, and relentless in attacking the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to establish control over the game by utilizing the Urzatron lands to generate large amounts of mana. This mana can then be used to cast powerful spells and abilities to disrupt the opponent's plans. The deck also includes a variety of artifacts that can be sacrificed for additional benefits, as well as creatures that can provide additional value. Ultimately, the goal is to outlast the opponent through a combination of control elements and resource management.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly to play powerful late game threats and control the board with removal spells. It utilizes the Urza lands to generate mana efficiently and cast big spells earlier than opponents. The deck also includes recursion with cards like Myr Retriever and Golem Foundry to maintain board presence. The goal is to outvalue opponents with card advantage and efficient mana usage while controlling the game until it can assemble a powerful win condition.
